name: HR Agent
description: Handles HR queries: leave (balance, history, policy, apply, approve, reject) and cubicle assignments.

skills.name: Get leave policy and how to apply
skills.description: Return the company leave policy — the leave types (Annual Leave, Sick Leave, Personal Leave), each with its rules (max days per year, minimum notice in days), and the information needed to submit a leave request (the leave type, the start and end dates in YYYY-MM-DD, an optional reason). Use this for purely informational questions: 'what leaves can I apply for', 'what's the leave policy', 'what are the leave rules', 'how do I apply for leave', 'what information do I need to apply for a leave'. Do NOT use this if the user actually wants to apply for leave (any expression of intent to apply / book / take leave, even without all the details) — use hr.apply_leave for that.

skills.name: Get leave balance
skills.description: Return the authenticated user's remaining leave balance (annual / sick / personal day counts).

skills.name: Get leave history
skills.description: Return the authenticated user's recent leave requests and their statuses.

skills.name: Apply for leave
skills.description: Submit a leave request for the authenticated user. Use this whenever the user wants to apply for / book / take / request leave — even if they have only given some of the details so far (e.g. just the dates, or just the type, or a follow-up like 'it will be a sick leave' after an earlier message gave the dates). It needs the leave type (one of: Annual Leave, Sick Leave, Personal Leave) and the start and end dates (YYYY-MM-DD); reason is optional. Provide whatever the conversation has supplied and omit the rest — the system will tell the user what's still missing. Compute relative dates from today's date given in the system prompt (e.g. 'next Monday', 'this Friday').

skills.name: Approve leave request
skills.description: Approve a pending leave request by its id (HR Admin only). Needs leave_id.

skills.name: Reject leave request
skills.description: Reject a pending leave request by its id with a reason (HR Admin only). Needs leave_id and reason.

skills.name: Cubicle vacancy summary
skills.description: Show the per-floor cubicle vacancy summary — total and vacant cubicles on each of the 4 floors (HR Admin). NOTE: 'cubicle', 'seat' and 'seating (arrangement)' all mean the same thing here — treat them identically. This is the STARTING POINT for allocating a cubicle/seat: use it whenever an HR admin wants to assign/allocate a cubicle/seat but hasn't named a floor or a specific seat yet — e.g. 'I need to allocate a cubicle', 'show me the cubicle assignments', 'what seats are free', 'cubicle allocation', 'the seating arrangement'. The reply asks which floor they want.

skills.name: List vacant cubicles on a floor
skills.description: Show the vacant (available) cubicle/seat ids on ONE specific floor (1-4) (HR Admin). ('cubicle' == 'seat'.) Use when the HR admin has named a floor — 'floor 3', "what's free on floor 2", 'show me the seats on floor 1' — including as a short-circuit straight from 'allocate a cubicle on floor 3'. The reply asks which seat (and for which employee). Needs `floor`.

skills.name: Assign a cubicle to an employee
skills.description: Assign ONE specific cubicle/seat to ONE specific employee — the FINAL step of cubicle/seat allocation (HR Admin, write). ('cubicle' == 'seat'.) Use ONLY when the message names BOTH a cubicle id (e.g. C-027 — a bare number like 'seat 27' means C-027) AND an employee (e.g. 'assign C-027 to jane.doe', 'give seat 14 to bob.smith'). If either is missing, use hr.cubicle_summary (no floor/seat picked yet) or hr.cubicle_list_floor (floor known) instead. Needs `cubicle_id` (the C-NNN id) and `employee_username`. Always put the employee identifier in `employee_username` regardless of whether it looks like a username or email address. Only also set `employee_email` if a separate email was explicitly provided in addition to a username.

skills.name: Get my cubicle
skills.description: Return the authenticated user's own cubicle assignment, if any.

skills.name: List all leave requests
skills.description: List all employees' leave requests; optional `status` filter (e.g. `Pending` for items awaiting approval) and optional `employee_name` filter. HR Admin only. Use for questions like 'show me all the leave requests', 'are there any leaves I need to approve', 'pending leave requests', 'leaves others submitted'.
